rationale.R1.2: The methodology declares LFP cells as the battery chemistry but then directly adopts per-cycle capacity-loss coefficients from Peterson et al. (2010), which characterize NMC chemistry. LFP and NMC have substantially different degradation kinetics; importing NMC coefficients into an LFP model without justification invalidates the quantitative degradation estimates underpinning the entire analysis.
rationale.R1.3: Table 2 reports $124/year regulation revenue against $187/year V2G-attributed degradation cost, a net of −$63/year per household. The conclusion states that 'RegRank delivers positive net returns to participating households.' This is a direct, load-bearing contradiction between the reported numbers and the paper's central empirical claim; any improvement that leaves it standing fails the task.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' centrally implicates prose quality — sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, elimination of redundancy, and precision of technical language throughout the excerpt.
rationale.R1.7: Peterson et al. (2010) is cited as the source of the degradation coefficients applied to LFP cells, but that paper studied NMC chemistry. Invoking the citation as direct authority for a different chemistry is a scholarship and attribution error that compounds the R1.2 methodology flaw.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement include correcting technical substance — logical errors, evidence–claim contradictions, and methodology mismatches — or is the intended scope limited to prose and writing quality only?
